title: Japanese Proverb of the Day on Success, “Fall down seven times, stand up eight.” True success is not the absence of failure, but the resilience to keep rising every time you fall—The ancient formula world needs right now
description: There is a Japanese proverb of the day on success that is only seven words long. Seven words that have outlasted empires, shaped samurai philosophy, and now sit framed in the offices of some of the world's most successful entrepreneurs. Nana korobi ya oki — "Fall down seven times, stand up eight." It sounds simple. It is not. Hidden inside that math is one of the most psychologically sophisticated success frameworks ever articulated by any culture, anywhere on Earth.

title: Rocky Mountain Power’s latest plan shows more investment in fossil fuels for the foreseeable future
description: Clean energy advocates decry no new investment in renewables, calling it contrary to Gov. Spencer Cox’s “Operation Gigawatt.”

title: State Urged to Build Fossil Park to Protect Ancient Fossils
description: Fossils are formed when an animal or plant dies and is preserved under layers of sediment. Over millions of years, the remains undergo a process of mineralisation or compression, leaving behind fossilised imprints or structures.

title: Oldest octopus fossil found to not be an octopus - Ars Technica
description: Oldest octopus fossil found to not be an octopus Ars Technica The ‘oldest octopus’ in the world isn’t an octopus after all, scientists find CNN Fossil Reexamination Upends Octopus Origin Story National Today This 300-million-year-old ‘octopus’ just lost its identity - scientists say it was never an octopus The Times of India Ancient fossil stripped of world's oldest octopus title in shock discovery Indy100

title: Oldest octopus fossil found to not be an octopus
description: Supposed “first octopus” was something else entirely.
